articles.title: Why Were These C.E.O.s in Beijing With Trump?
articles.summary: Some of America’s most powerful C.E.O.s accompanied President Trump to Beijing during his summit with President Xi Jinping of China. Our reporter Ana Swanson explains what they were hoping to gain fro

articles.title: Trump’s ‘Learning Curve’ on China Ends With Conciliation at Summit
articles.summary: The president has shifted the foundations of American policy toward China, throwing aside the adversarial approach of recent years.

articles.title: SCOTUS upholds abortion pill telehealth access. And, Trump returns from China visit
articles.summary: The Supreme Court kept access to the abortion pill mifepristone available via telehealth. And, Trump says he made multiple trade deals during his state visit to China.

articles.title: Key takeaways from Trump's China trip
articles.summary: President Trump returns to the U.S. after wrapping up his whirlwind trip to China.

articles.title: China economist shares takeaways from the Trump-Xi summit
articles.summary: Steve Inskeep speaks with Chinese economist Keyu Jin about what came out of the Trump-Xi summit.

articles.title: How the relationship between the U.S. and China has changed under Trump
articles.summary: NPR's Steve Inskeep talks with Chinese scholar Da Wei about the history of the U.S.-China relationship and how it has changed under President Trump.

articles.title: Supreme Court Rejects Virginia Democrats’ Effort to Reinstate New Voting Map
articles.summary: State officials had asked the justices to step in to allow the state to use a congressional map in the midterms that was drawn by Democrats and recently approved by voters.

articles.title: With Possible Raúl Castro Indictment, U.S. Eyes Venezuela Playbook
articles.summary: Amid stepped-up surveillance flights, a visit of the C.I.A. director and an energy embargo, the White House is trying to increase pressure on Cuba.

articles.title: Supreme Court is death knell for Virginia's Democratic-friendly congressional maps
articles.summary: The new map was drawn by Democrats and approved by Virginia voters, but the state Supreme Court declared the referendum null and void because lawmakers failed to follow the proper procedures to get th

articles.title: The Push to Indict Raúl Castro, Cuba’s Former President
articles.summary: Mr. Castro, Cuba’s former president, could soon face charges in the 1996 killings of four volunteer airmen who were members of a humanitarian group that searched for migrants at sea.

articles.title: Iran-Backed Commander Accused of Plotting U.S. Attacks
articles.summary: Also, the Eurovision finale. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.

articles.title: Iran has been confiscating property from critics of the regime
articles.summary: Iran's government has been confiscating property from people it deems traitors or critics of the regime.

articles.title: Photos: Cuba Plunges Into Darkness as U.S. Cuts Off Oil Supply
articles.summary: The United States has choked off Cuba’s fuel supply, plunging the already impoverished island into an acute energy crisis.

articles.title: CIA director's visit to Cuba echoes past memories of U.S. in Latin America
articles.summary: CIA Director John Ratcliffe makes a surprise visit to Cuba as the spy agency calls on the nation to make "fundamental changes." Cuba says it has run out of fuel and is suffering massive blackouts.

articles.title: Colorado's Democratic governor will let Trump ally Tina Peters out of prison early
articles.summary: Gov. Jared Polis' controversial commutation follows a pressure campaign by the Trump administration to free Tina Peters, an ex-county official who was convicted of tampering with election equipment.

articles.title: Texas Supreme Court Rejects Abbott Effort to Remove Democrats After Walkout
articles.summary: Republican state leaders tried to expel Democratic lawmakers during their fight over redistricting last year. The court said it was not necessary.

articles.title: Hantavirus countdown: U.S. cruise passengers settle in for 42 days of waiting
articles.summary: The hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship has potentially exposed passengers to a deadly disease. Most returning Americans are now housed in Nebraska. Some may be cleared to quarantine at home.

articles.title: Shakira returns to official World Cup song duty, this time with Burna Boy
articles.summary: FIFA's official 2026 World Cup song is "Dai Dai" from Shakira and Burna Boy. There are a number of factors that shape which songs define a tournament — and endure beyond it.

articles.title: The Trump administration is planning a prayer event on the National Mall. All but one of the speaker
articles.summary: The event comes as a new survey finds that many Americans aren't comfortable mixing religion and politics.

articles.title: Harvey Weinstein's third sex crimes trial in New York ends in mistrial
articles.summary: It was the second time in a year a jury was unable to reach a verdict on a rape charge brought by Jessica Mann.

articles.title: Can the NFL's Rooney Rule survive the DEI backlash?
articles.summary: Florida's attorney general says the NFL's Rooney Rule, which requires teams to interview minority candidates for top jobs, is discriminatory. Trump's EEOC has challenged such policies elsewhere.

articles.title: Death toll in attack on Kyiv apartment building now stands at 24
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy led an official day of mourning in Kyiv a day after a Russian cruise missile flattened an apartment building. It was one of the deadliest attacks on the capital

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
